commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 23 July 2026

US equities dominate the liquidity hierarchy: Nasdaq 100 leads decisively with an ILLIQ of 0.0028% per $1M traded and 0.7480% AR spread, while NYSE follows at 0.0056% per $1M and 0.6386% spread, together commanding $544.3M in comparable volume. Asia-Pacific exchanges occupy the mid-tier, with Tokyo Stock Exchange ranking third despite elevated ILLIQ of 0.0339% per $1M, offset by the highest turnover ratio at 2.9535%, while Hong Kong Stock Exchange and Euronext cluster tightly with ILLIQ ranging 0.0301–0.0364% per $1M. The laggard tier—SIX Swiss, Korea Exchange, Xetra, and London Stock Exchange—all exhibit ILLIQ above 0.0445% per $1M, with Korea Exchange standing out as notably illiquid at 0.1430% per $1M and a 1.5425% spread, yet maintaining modest $15.3M comparable volume. A critical divergence appears at SIX Swiss, where the tightest AR spread at 0.5053% masks the sixth-worst ILLIQ metric of 0.0445% per $1M and anemic 0.2386% turnover, signalling that quoted tightness obscures meaningful execution slippage in low-volume regimes. London Stock Exchange similarly shows this pattern: competitive 0.8038% spread belied by the worst ILLIQ at 0.0795% per $1M.
